desc: Chengdu blends laid-back tea-house culture with major heritage sites and one of China's strongest food scenes, while also functioning as a gateway to western Sichuan mountain itineraries. Spring and autumn are generally the easiest windows for comfort and visibility; midsummer is hot-humid with heavier rain, and national-holiday weeks can create sharp domestic demand spikes.
hls: Giant Panda Base and conservation centres
Outstanding Sichuan food culture (hot pot, mapo tofu, noodles)
Historic lanes, teahouses, and relaxed urban pace
Strong base for Leshan, Mount Emei, and wider western Sichuan
Deep archaeology via Sanxingdui and Jinsha heritage
Good value for long-stay city exploration

todo.name: Giant Panda Breeding Research Base
todo.desc: Arrive by 08:00 to see pandas most active. The experience is genuinely magical.

todo.name: Leshan Giant Buddha
todo.desc: World's largest stone Buddha (71 m), carved into a clifftop above three rivers. UNESCO World Heritage.

todo.name: Mount Emei
todo.desc: Sacred Buddhist mountain with ancient temple complexes ascending through cloud forest to 3,099 m. One of China's Four Sacred Buddhist Mountains.

todo.name: Jiuzhaigou Valley
todo.desc: Unesco-listed valley of impossibly turquoise lakes and waterfalls. Best in autumn for foliage. ~6 hr from Chengdu.

todo.name: Sanxingdui Museum
todo.desc: Bronze masks from a 3,000-year-old civilisation — one of the great archaeological wonders of Asia.

todo.name: Wuhou Shrine & Jinli Street
todo.desc: 3rd-century memorial adjacent to a beautifully restored Qing dynasty street of teahouses and craft shops.

todo.name: Kuan Zhai Alley
todo.desc: Restored Qing-era courtyard lanes — Chengdu's most charming historic district.

todo.name: Sichuan Opera (face-changing)
todo.desc: The mysterious bianlian performance is a Chengdu institution — best seen at a traditional teahouse theatre.

wifi.note: Core hotel connectivity is usable, but many Western platforms/services are blocked by the Great Firewall. Set up and test any required business access tools before arrival.

prac.fltNote: No direct London departures; common UK routings connect via Doha, Dubai, Hong Kong, or major mainland hubs. Return fares are often ~£420–£650 off-peak, ~£650–£900 in shoulder demand, and ~£900+ around Chinese New Year, Golden Week, and summer peaks.
prac.visa: China entry rules vary by nationality and current bilateral policy; verify latest visa/visa-free terms before booking.
alerts: Platform restrictions can affect navigation, messaging, and payment apps; prepare alternatives in advance.
Golden Week and Chinese New Year periods can sharply increase transport/accommodation pressure.
Summer humidity and rain can reduce comfort for long outdoor days.

sweet: March–May and October–November are typically the strongest balance of weather, visibility, and crowd pressure outside holiday spikes.
